ArchitectureNotes.objective: Deliver a graded Phase 1 marketplace prototype without blocking Phase 2 multi-merchant, RBAC, agent, or search expansion.
Keep backend runtime fully async, preserve strict FSD boundaries on the frontend, and expose both human and machine-readable commerce interfaces.
ArchitectureNotes.non-goal: No application code is generated in Phase 0; only structure, contracts, and planning artifacts are produced.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-1: Async database and S3 boundaries must remain pure or the assignment fails on runtime constraints.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-2: Phase 2 merchant scope can be blocked later if seller, merchant, and admin concepts are not separated in the schema now.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-3: Agent search credibility depends on explicit interpretation headers and audit-ready traces, not just filtered results.

contract.purpose: Define the GRACE operating constitution and keep planning artifacts coherent across repos.

export-engineering-protocol.PURPOSE: Project-wide engineering rules and semantic markup reference.

notes.note-1: Backend repo owns shared GRACE documents for the full Kodex platform.

contract.purpose: Declare the local environment contract for database, storage, auth, CORS, and feature flags.

notes.note-1: Secrets remain placeholders in version control and become concrete only in local .env files.

contract.purpose: Run the local Kodex development topology with health-gated startup and bind-mounted data.

export-compose-stack.PURPOSE: PostgreSQL, MinIO, backend, and frontend development stack.

notes.note-1: Backend must wait on postgres health, while MinIO only needs service-start readiness.

contract.purpose: Load and validate backend runtime settings from environment variables via Pydantic Settings.

notes.note-1: All secrets, URLs, and feature flags flow through this module.

contract.purpose: Create async SQLAlchemy engine and session factories for FastAPI dependencies and seed workflows.

export-get_session.PURPOSE: Yield AsyncSession dependencies for handlers and services.

notes.note-1: Request paths may use async SQLAlchemy only; sync calls are forbidden.

contract.purpose: Provide admin password verification, JWT issuing, JWT decoding, and future role/merchant claim helpers.

export-login_admin.PURPOSE: Authenticate platform admin and create Bearer JWT.

notes.note-1: JWT schema must remain compatible with future merchant_admin role and merchant_id claim.

contract.purpose: Define SQLAlchemy models for products, sellers, offers, and product attributes.

export-product.PURPOSE: Product model with merchant_id, status, and search_vector prep.

notes.note-1: nearest_delivery_date is computed in queries, not persisted on the product row.

contract.purpose: Define SQLAlchemy models for merchants and agent request audit records required for Phase 2 preparation.

export-merchant.PURPOSE: Merchant model used for Phase 1 demo ownership and Phase 2 RBAC scope.

notes.note-1: agent_requests persists endpoint, query, interpreted_intent, result_count, response_ms, and created_at.

contract.purpose: Materialize the Phase 1 schema, including PHASE_2_PREP columns and indexes, through Alembic CLI migrations.

notes.note-1: search_vector uses a russian tsvector generated column with a GIN index.

contract.purpose: Define the frontend runtime contract, build tooling, lint rules, Docker image, and environment defaults.

export-tooling.PURPOSE: package.json, Vite, TypeScript, ESLint, Docker, and env bootstrap.

notes.note-1: Owns the static verification surface required before any frontend slice can be trusted.

contract.purpose: Provide the single typed HTTP client and shared agent/public/admin API helpers for the frontend.

export-client.PURPOSE: Shared axios instance with auth and error interceptors.

notes.note-1: No component or entity may issue HTTP outside this module.

contract.purpose: Provide shared UI primitives that can be reused across features, widgets, and pages without leaking business logic.

export-button.PURPOSE: Shared button primitive with typed props and emits.

notes.note-1: Contains no data fetching or slice-specific business rules.

contract.purpose: Provide pure formatting and low-level helpers shared across the frontend.

export-formatters.PURPOSE: formatPrice and formatDate helpers with stable RU locale output.

notes.note-1: Pure helpers only; no imports from entities, features, widgets, or pages.

contract.purpose: Query products for public catalog, detail, and agent search use cases without N+1 behavior.

export-list_paginated.PURPOSE: Cursor-paginated product listing with nearest delivery date scalar subquery.

notes.note-1: Future merchant scoping hooks are added here by merchant_id filters.

contract.purpose: Load offer collections for product detail and admin write workflows with stable ordering options.

export-list_for_product.PURPOSE: Return offers sorted by price or delivery date.

notes.note-1: Sorting must be deterministic to keep modal toggles stable without full page reloads.

contract.purpose: Persist admin CRUD changes for products, sellers, offers, and media object keys.

export-save_product_image_keys.PURPOSE: Persist original and thumbnail object key references on products.

notes.note-1: Repository surface remains platform-admin scoped in Phase 1 and becomes merchant-aware later.

contract.purpose: Upload original product images and generated thumbnails to MinIO using aioboto3 and Pillow.

export-upload_product_image.PURPOSE: Store original plus 300x300 JPEG thumbnail and return presigned URLs.

notes.note-1: Only aioboto3 is allowed in request paths; sync boto3 is forbidden.

contract.purpose: Define typed product contracts and public/admin product API adapters for catalog, detail, and admin views.

export-product-types.PURPOSE: Typed product list/detail/admin contracts and API helpers.

notes.note-1: Must remain self-contained and avoid importing other entity slices to preserve strict FSD discipline.

contract.purpose: Define typed offer contracts and sort semantics shared by widgets and features.

notes.note-1: Provides only public types; data fetching remains in the product entity API.

contract.purpose: Define typed seller contracts for offers and admin table rendering.

export-seller-types.PURPOSE: Seller public types for offer and page composition.

notes.note-1: Purely structural; no network or browser-side behavior lives here.

contract.purpose: Assemble public catalog and product detail responses from repository data and response schemas.

export-list_products.PURPOSE: Return cursor-based product listing payload with next cursor metadata.

notes.note-1: Public list items and agent search items share the same projection shape.

contract.purpose: Apply offer sorting rules and response shaping for product detail and admin views.

export-sort_offers.PURPOSE: Sort offer collections by price or delivery date.

notes.note-1: Sorting rules must stay consistent with frontend SortButtons semantics.

contract.purpose: Coordinate admin CRUD operations, image uploads, and product media metadata persistence.

export-upload_product_image.PURPOSE: Persist original and thumbnail object keys after MinIO upload.

notes.note-1: Image upload is the highest-risk admin path because it spans storage, Pillow, and DB persistence.

contract.purpose: Interpret natural-language product queries, query catalog data, and record audit-ready agent search metadata.

export-search_products.PURPOSE: Parse intent and return ProductListItem-compatible search results.

notes.note-1: X-Query-Interpreted and agent_requests are the evidence surfaces for intent parsing correctness.

contract.purpose: Implement IntersectionObserver-based catalog pagination state and sentinel lifecycle.

notes.note-1: Must prevent concurrent page loads and expose deterministic reset behavior for agent search results.

contract.purpose: Implement offer sorting state and the sort button control used by the product modal.

notes.note-1: SortButtons owns no fetching logic; it only emits the selected public sort value.

contract.purpose: Implement admin login/logout state, token persistence, and route redirection behavior.

export-admin-auth.PURPOSE: useAdminAuth composable for login, logout, and auth status.

notes.note-1: Owns token lifecycle but delegates actual request construction to shared/api/client.ts.

contract.purpose: Implement Web Speech API voice capture, graceful fallback, and typed agent-search integration.

notes.note-1: When speech support is absent, the feature must degrade silently and keep text search available.

contract.purpose: Expose public catalog and product detail endpoints for anonymous visitors and frontend consumers.

export-get_products.PURPOSE: GET /v1/public/products list endpoint with cursor pagination.

notes.note-1: All route handlers remain async def with awaited service calls only.

contract.purpose: Expose the admin login endpoint that validates credentials and returns a Bearer JWT.

notes.note-1: Future merchant auth reuses the same auth primitives but different claims and scopes.

contract.purpose: Expose admin CRUD endpoints for products, sellers, offers, and product image uploads.

notes.note-1: Phase 1 keeps admin scope platform-wide; merchant scoping is injected later beneath this surface.

contract.purpose: Expose llms.txt, capability context, and natural-language search endpoints for external LLM agents.

notes.note-1: GET /llms.txt is treated as part of the agent discovery surface even though it sits at the root path.

contract.purpose: Render catalog product cards with thumbnail, pricing, stock, and nearest delivery data.

notes.note-1: Pure rendering plus select emit; modal state lives outside the widget.

contract.purpose: Render product details in a modal and re-fetch offers when the selected sort changes.

notes.note-1: Owns detail fetching and sort-triggered re-fetch; fetches only when a product id is present.

contract.purpose: Render the admin products table and emit edit, delete, and image-upload actions.

notes.note-1: Emits actions only; all mutations remain page or feature-owned.

contract.purpose: Assemble the public catalog page, agent search bar, infinite scroll grid, and thin product fallback page.

export-catalog-page.PURPOSE: Catalog page and product fallback public exports.

notes.note-1: Owns list reset after agent search and selection state for the modal.

contract.purpose: Assemble the admin login page and authenticated admin catalog management page.

notes.note-1: Protects admin data loading behind token checks and shared client auth interceptors.

contract.purpose: Wire FastAPI application startup, middleware, routers, CORS, and root discovery endpoints.

export-app.PURPOSE: FastAPI application entry point for uvicorn.

notes.note-1: CORS must explicitly allow localhost:5173 for Phase 1 frontend development.

contract.purpose: Populate a fresh database with demo merchant, products, sellers, attributes, offers, and valid delivery dates.

export-run_seed.PURPOSE: Seed database and storage references for local/demo environments.

notes.note-1: delivery_date generation must always use today() + randint(0,6) and assign products to the demo merchant.

contract.purpose: Wire Vue app bootstrap, Pinia, Router, page shells, and global providers.

export-bootstrap.PURPOSE: Vue application bootstrap and route registration.

notes.note-1: Owns app bootstrap and route guards only; page behavior stays in page modules.

contract.purpose: Enable merchant-scoped self-service catalog management on top of the Phase 1 prepared schema.

notes.note-1: Planned after Phase 1 once merchant JWTs and scoped admin UI are introduced.

contract.purpose: Centralize role and merchant scope checks shared by future merchant and support actors.

notes.note-1: Phase 1 JWT shape must already accommodate this future module.

contract.purpose: Extend catalog retrieval with richer full-text, semantic, and ranking strategies beyond Phase 1 intent parsing.

export-run_search.PURPOSE: Future search orchestration beyond the base product repository filters.

notes.note-1: Phase 1 search_vector and GIN index are laid down specifically for this expansion path.

contract.purpose: Introduce optional recommendation engine integration behind disabled-by-default feature flags.

notes.note-1: Feature flags already exist in the environment contract, but stay disabled in Phase 1.

contract.purpose: Support post-ТЗ order placement, fulfillment, and merchant operational workflows.

export-create_order.PURPOSE: Future order creation and lifecycle surface.

notes.note-1: Explicitly excluded from Phase 1, but reserved in the roadmap for the broader marketplace platform.

contract.purpose: Expand Phase 1 agent_requests persistence into richer analytics, replay, and operational tracing.

export-record_agent_event.PURPOSE: Future richer audit/event writer over the prepared agent_requests table.

notes.note-1: Phase 1 writes the minimal audit shape; Phase 2 enriches it with analytics and replay semantics.

DF-001.NAME: VisitorBrowseCatalog
DF-001.step-1: PublicApi validates cursor and delegates to ProductService.
DF-001.step-2: ProductService calls ProductRepository.list_paginated() with scalar subquery MIN(offers.delivery_date).
DF-001.step-3: ProductRepository returns items ordered for stable cursor pagination without N+1 queries.
DF-001.step-4: PublicApi returns {items, next_cursor} plus X-Total-Count header.

DF-002.NAME: VisitorViewProductDetail
DF-002.step-1: PublicApi resolves a product by id through ProductService.
DF-002.step-2: ProductService loads full product projection and OfferService.sorted_offers for default price ordering.
DF-002.step-3: Frontend feature SortButtons toggles client-side sort requests without full page reload.

DF-003.NAME: AdminAuthenticate
DF-003.step-1: AdminAuthApi validates request payload and delegates to Auth.login_admin.
DF-003.step-2: Auth verifies password against env-configured credentials and issues a JWT with expiry.
DF-003.step-3: Frontend app stores the Bearer token and injects it into admin requests.

DF-004.NAME: AdminUploadImage
DF-004.step-1: AdminCatalogApi enforces Bearer auth and delegates to AdminCatalogService.
DF-004.step-2: AdminCatalogService invokes S3Service.upload_product_image() to upload original and thumbnail objects.
DF-004.step-3: AdminCatalogRepository persists image_object_key and thumbnail_object_key on the product.
DF-004.step-4: AdminCatalogApi returns presigned URLs for both stored assets.

DF-005.NAME: SeedFreshDatabase
DF-005.step-1: Seed bootstraps demo merchant creation through PlatformModels.
DF-005.step-2: Seed creates 100+ products, 5-8 sellers, 2-6 attributes, and 2-8 offers per product.
DF-005.step-4: Seed links every product to the Kodex Demo Store merchant_id.

DF-006.NAME: AgentSearchProducts
DF-006.step-1: AgentApi accepts natural-language query and delegates to AgentService.
DF-006.step-2: AgentService parses name, max_price, delivery, and stock intents into explicit filter structure.
DF-006.step-3: ProductRepository returns ProductListItem-compatible results using the interpreted filters.
DF-006.step-4: AgentApi responds with results and X-Query-Interpreted header, while agent_requests captures the audit entry.

DF-007.NAME: FutureMerchantScopedAccess
DF-007.step-1: RBAC validates role=merchant_admin and merchant_id claim.
DF-007.step-2: MerchantPortal delegates scoped catalog operations through AdminCatalogRepository and ProductRepository filters.
DF-007.step-3: Any cross-merchant access attempt is denied before data leaves the service layer.

Phase-0.name: Bootstrap
Phase-0.goal: Project identity, governance, infra bootstrap, and planning artifacts exist before any code generation wave.
Phase-0.step-1: Create AGENTS.md and shared GRACE documents in kodex-backend.
Phase-0.step-2: Define .env.example for PostgreSQL, MinIO, auth, CORS, and feature flags.
Phase-0.step-3: Bootstrap Docker Compose with healthcheck-gated backend startup and hot reload volumes.

Phase-1.name: CorePlatformBackend
Phase-1.goal: Implement the graded backend prototype with schema foresight for multi-merchant expansion.
Phase-1.step-1: Implement backend settings and env validation.
Phase-1.step-2: Implement async database engine and session dependency.
Phase-1.step-3: Implement admin auth and future-ready JWT claims.
Phase-1.step-4: Define catalog ORM models with merchant_id, status, and search_vector fields.
Phase-1.step-5: Define merchants and agent_requests ORM models.
Phase-1.step-6: Create Phase 1 schema migrations including PHASE_2_PREP artifacts.
Phase-1.step-7: Implement cursor pagination and detail queries without N+1 behavior.
Phase-1.step-8: Implement offer read and sorting query helpers.
Phase-1.step-10: Implement async MinIO upload and thumbnail generation.
Phase-1.step-13: Implement admin catalog service and image persistence flow.
Phase-1.step-14: Implement natural-language query interpretation and audit persistence.
Phase-1.step-15: Implement public catalog and product detail routes.
Phase-1.step-17: Implement admin CRUD and image upload routes.
Phase-1.step-18: Implement llms.txt, capability context, and agent search routes.
Phase-1.step-19: Wire FastAPI app, CORS, and router registration.
Phase-1.step-20: Implement realistic seed script with demo merchant ownership.

Phase-1b.name: FrontendAndAgentUx
Phase-1b.goal: Implement FSD frontend layers, public catalog UX, admin auth, and agent-first interactions.
Phase-1b.step-1: Establish package, Vite, TypeScript, ESLint, Docker, env, and HTML tooling files.
Phase-1b.step-2: Create the shared axios client and agent/public/admin API wrappers.
Phase-1b.step-3: Create shared UI primitives for buttons, badges, and loading states.
Phase-1b.step-4: Create shared pure formatting helpers and public exports.
Phase-1b.step-5: Implement product types and public/admin product API adapters.
Phase-1b.step-6: Implement offer types and public sort exports.
Phase-1b.step-7: Implement seller types and public exports.
Phase-1b.step-8: Implement the infinite scroll composable and sentinel lifecycle.
Phase-1b.step-9: Implement offer sort state and SortButtons.
Phase-1b.step-10: Implement admin auth state, token persistence, and redirects.
Phase-1b.step-11: Implement voice search with graceful Web Speech API fallback.
Phase-1b.step-12: Implement the catalog product card widget.
Phase-1b.step-13: Implement the product detail modal with sort-triggered re-fetch.
Phase-1b.step-14: Implement the admin products table widget and action emits.
Phase-1b.step-15: Assemble the public catalog page, agent search bar, and product fallback page.
Phase-1b.step-16: Assemble the login page and authenticated admin management page.
Phase-1b.step-17: Bootstrap the Vue app, router, Pinia, and route guards.

Phase-2.name: PostTzRoadmap
Phase-2.goal: Expand Kodex into a full multi-merchant Agentic Commerce platform without reworking Phase 1 foundations.
Phase-2.step-1: Implement centralized role and merchant scope enforcement.
Phase-2.step-2: Implement merchant self-service portal and scoped admin services.
Phase-2.step-3: Expand agent audit persistence and replay-ready traces.
Phase-2.step-4: Upgrade search beyond simple interpreted filters.
Phase-2.step-5: Integrate optional recommendation engine behind feature flags.
Phase-2.step-6: Introduce orders and downstream marketplace workflows.

ExecutionPolicy.worker-owns: Only approved source files and module-local tests inside the current phase write scope.
Backend execution must respect the layer chain config/database → models/auth → repositories/S3 → services → APIs → app/seed.
Frontend execution must proceed shared → entities → features → widgets → pages → app with public-API-only imports.
